extension.title: Copilot Scheduler
config.showNotifications: Show notifications when tasks are executed
config.executionHistoryLimit: Number of recent execution history entries to keep (10–500)
config.language: UI language (applies to extension Webview/Tree UI; settings-description updates may require window reload)
config.timezone: Timezone for cron scheduling (e.g., 'Asia/Tokyo'). Leave empty for system default.
config.chatSession: Default chat session behavior. Tasks can override this.
config.chatSession.new: Create new chat session for each execution (more isolated, usually slower)
config.chatSession.continue: Continue in existing chat session (usually faster)
config.autoModeDefault: Default value for new tasks' auto-mode hint (inserts an autonomous-execution instruction at the beginning of the runtime prompt). To automatically keep AI-applied edits, configure [chat.editing.autoAcceptDelay](#chat.editing.autoAcceptDelay#). If the link does not open, search for chat.editing.autoAcceptDelay in Settings.
config.commandDelayFactor: Delay multiplier for Copilot command sequencing (0.1–2.0). Lower is faster but may reduce stability in some environments.
config.manualRunNextRunPolicy: How to compute next run after Run Now
config.manualRunNextRunPolicy.advance: Advance from existing next run when possible (keeps queue progression)
config.manualRunNextRunPolicy.fromNow: Recalculate from current time (ignores existing future next run)
config.jitterSeconds: Default max random delay in seconds before running a task (0 = off). Each task can override this.
config.maxDailyExecutions: Maximum scheduled executions per day across all tasks (0 = unlimited, 1–100). ⚠️ Unlimited may risk API rate-limiting.
config.minimumIntervalWarning: Show a warning when a cron interval is shorter than 30 minutes
config.globalPromptsPath: Custom path for global prompt templates. Default: VS Code's User/prompts folder (Windows: %APPDATA%/Code/User/prompts, macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/Code/User/prompts, Linux: $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/Code/User/prompts or ~/.config/Code/User/prompts)
config.globalAgentsPath: Custom path for global agent files (*.agent.md). Default: auto-detect VS Code's User/prompts folder plus ~/.copilot/agents (Windows: %USERPROFILE%/.copilot/agents). Setting this overrides the default discovery roots.
config.reportIssue: 🐛 Found a bug? [Report an issue on GitHub](https://github.com/aktsmm/vscode-copilot-scheduler/issues)
config.lmTools.enableWriteTools: Allow Copilot Chat / agent mode to create, update, delete and toggle scheduler tasks via language-model tools. Read tools are always available. When disabled, only read tools are exposed.
config.lmTools.confirmationMode: Controls the extension-provided custom confirmation messages for Copilot Chat language-model write tools. VS Code or Copilot Chat may still show a generic approval dialog.
config.lmTools.confirmationMode.always: Always show custom confirmation messages from the extension for create, update, delete and enable-disable tools.
config.lmTools.confirmationMode.destruct: Show custom confirmation messages from the extension only for destructive operations such as delete.
config.lmTools.confirmationMode.minimal: Suppress all extension-provided custom confirmation messages. VS Code or Copilot Chat may still show generic approval.
tool.scheduler_query.modelDescription: Query Copilot Scheduler for task information without side effects. Set 'kind' to one of: 'list' (all tasks, optionally filtered by scope and enabled state), 'get' (single task by id), 'history' (recent execution history, optionally filtered by taskId), or 'preview_cron' (next occurrences of a cron expression; returns times in the configured timezone). Always use this before making changes to confirm which task the user means.
tool.scheduler_create_task.modelDescript: Use when the user asks to schedule, set up, register, or automate a recurring Copilot prompt/task. Create a new scheduled Copilot task. Requires: name, cronExpression, prompt, and scope ('global' or 'workspace'). Optional fields include promptSource ('inline' | 'local' | 'global') and promptPath when promptSource is local or global. New tasks can be created disabled and enabled later via scheduler_set_task_enabled. Enabling a task requires the user to have accepted the first-time execution disclaimer.
tool.scheduler_update_task.modelDescript: Use when the user asks to change, edit, reschedule, or revise an existing scheduled Copilot task. Update fields of an existing task. Do NOT pass 'enabled' here – use scheduler_set_task_enabled instead (the tool will refuse the update otherwise). Missing fields keep their current value.
tool.scheduler_delete_task.modelDescript: Use when the user asks to delete, remove, or cancel an existing scheduled Copilot task. Delete a task by id. This is permanent and cannot be undone. The user will be shown the task name, scope and workspace in the confirmation dialog before the tool actually runs.
tool.scheduler_set_task_enabled.modelDes: Use when the user asks to enable, disable, pause, resume, turn on, or turn off an existing scheduled Copilot task. Enable or disable a task by id. Enabling requires the user to have accepted the first-time execution disclaimer in the Copilot Scheduler view.
